title: anisodontea racemosa
intro: Anisodontea racemosa, also known as the white wild marwadza or the bushveld anisodontea, is a flowering plant species belonging to the mallow family. Traditionally, Anisodontea racemosa is used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it a valuable remedy for treating various health conditions. The plant's leaves, roots, and stems are used to prepare herbal remedies that can aid in reducing fever, soothing skin irritations, and alleviating respiratory issues. Anisodontea racemosa is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and can also be used topically in the form of a poultice or ointment.
